audience: Top-producing real estate agents and service companies in Marion County. Professional, data-driven, competitive. They want market intel and tools that give them an edge. 850-1000 copies mailed monthly.
layout_personality: Clean, bold, data-forward. Premium business publication feel. Structured grids with stat callouts. Authority badges and market data prominent. Aerial and architectural photography. Two-column editorial for feature articles.
cover_treatment: Large bold Comfortaa all caps WHITE on full-bleed dark aerial or architectural photography. 'The' in Grand Hotel script above masthead. GET ROOTED MEDIA / MARION COUNTY / ISSUE DATE in small caps top left. No teal bar on cover — the dark photo IS the background.
photography_treatment: Aerial neighborhood photography, professional real estate settings, closing table meetings, handshakes, warm rural Florida landscapes from above. Bold drone shots. Each image should feel like it belongs in a regional business magazine. Dark moody treatment acceptable for editorial gravitas.
color_application: Near-black #1A1A1A for dark sections and cover. Teal #52B5CB for stat callouts, accent bars, and teal pull quote text. Warm Cream #FAF8F4 for body sections. White for all type on dark. NO dusty rose. NO sage green.
typography_application: Comfortaa all caps for all section headers and display. Grand Hotel for 'The' script. Merriweather Light for body articles. Nunito for captions, stats labels, bylines. Pull quotes in Merriweather Italic teal.
whitespace_approach: Generous whitespace between data sections. Stat callouts need breathing room. Card grids with clear gutters. Professional restraint — every element earns its place. White space signals premium.
editorial_voice: Voice 4: The Closing Table. Sharp, clean, insight-driven. Contrarian observations. Data points. Monday-morning takeaways. Never puff pieces. Never open with job title.
pull_quote_style: Merriweather Italic in teal #52B5CB. No quotation marks styled as decorative elements — let the italic and color do the work.
cta_style: Dark near-black button with white Comfortaa text OR teal button with white text. Never use dusty rose or sage green for CT CTAs.

audience: New homeowners in Marion County within first 90 days. Community-oriented, family-focused, excited about their new home. They want local tips, neighborhood guides, and connection. 2500+ copies per quarterly issue.
layout_personality: Warm, welcoming, community-forward. Premium lifestyle magazine feel. Photo-driven layouts with generous image space. Dusty rose section backgrounds for lifestyle features. More relaxed visual rhythm than Closing Table. Family scenes and community photography lead.
cover_treatment: White background cover with large Comfortaa all caps masthead. Teal O and Warm Brown O in FRONT PORCH logo lettering — this is a fixed design element. 'The' in Grand Hotel script above. Full-bleed family or community photography takes up majority of cover. Teaser headlines in Comfortaa bold at bottom.
photography_treatment: Welcoming home exteriors, Florida neighborhoods, families arriving at homes, porch scenes with families and dogs, community parks, neighborhood streets. Warm golden Florida light. Every image should feel like a warm welcome. No aerial shots — keep it human and ground-level.
color_application: Warm Cream #FAF8F4 as primary page background. Dusty Rose #E0A6A6 for lifestyle section backgrounds — used as full bleed background color. Teal #52B5CB for CTAs and friendly accent touches. Sage Green #78AD4E for pull quote italic text only. Near-black #1A1A1A for grounding headers used more sparingly than CT.
typography_application: Same font families as CT but softer application. Comfortaa for headers in title case more often than all caps. Grand Hotel for 'The' script. Merriweather for feature body text. Nunito for guide content and practical tips. Pull quotes in Merriweather Italic sage green.
whitespace_approach: More breathing room than Closing Table. Lifestyle photos get generous space. Content blocks feel relaxed but organized. Less data density and more visual storytelling. Friendly pacing — never rushed.
editorial_voice: Voice 1: The Front Porch. Warm, people-first, scene-driven. Sensory details. Let subjects speak in their own words. Voice 3: The Welcome Mat for practical guide content. Never corporate language.
pull_quote_style: Merriweather Italic in sage green #78AD4E on cream or dusty rose backgrounds. Warm and inviting.
cta_style: Teal button with white Comfortaa text. Warm and approachable language on CTAs — never urgent or pushy.
